title: Apparmor fixes
body:
- don't load/set the aa profile when initializing the container
- add a baseline test to avoid regressing on privileged containers

comments.body:

We should throw an error if the user specifies

--security-opt apparmor=... and --privileged at the same time.

> We should throw an error if the user specifies
> 
> --security-opt apparmor=... and --privileged at the same time.

Do we have such checks in place for selinux?

> > We should throw an error if the user specifies
> > --security-opt apparmor=... and --privileged at the same time.
> 
> Do we have such checks in place for selinux?

Just checked, we don't check that for selinux either. Not sure if adding the check will cause regressions for some users.
Well we could add a warning.  Since --privileged will override any --security-opt selection.  Or any --cap-add/cap-drop selection.

> Well we could add a warning. Since --privileged will override any --security-opt selection. Or any --cap-add/cap-drop selection.

Done :heavy_check_mark::
```
[libpod (apparmor-fixes)]$ sudo ./bin/podman --log-level=warn create --privileged --security-opt label=foo alpine ls
WARN[0000] setting security options with --privileged has no effect 
7218ea3309a8c5c811b4cd75d58fc8ada7b33f80316ebf0d8019cd3f15309897
```

@rhatdan should be happy now. @baude, @mheon, @giuseppe PTAL.

lets merge ... @vrothberg can you follow up on CI and apparmor?

/lgtm

> lets merge ... @vrothberg can you follow up on CI and apparmor?

Thanks! Sure, will do.
